title: WorkflowRuntimeState.GetStartEvent().GetVersion() always returns an empty version
body:
## In what area(s)?

/area runtime

## What version of Dapr?

 1.1.17

## Expected Behavior

calling `WorkflowRuntimeState.GetStartEvent().GetVersion()` returns the workflow version.

## Actual Behavior

Reading `WorkflowRuntimeState.GetStartEvent().GetVersion()` returns nil for any workflow scheduled through the standard client API. 

The runtime-resolved version is **not** stored on ExecutionStartedEvent (the event returned by `WorkflowRuntimeState.GetStartEvent`).

**AI generated analysis**: 

`ExecutionStartedEvent.Version` is only populated when a client schedules with an explicit version on `CreateInstanceRequest`. The standard durabletask-go ScheduleNewWorkflow API has no WithVersion option, and even when `CreateInstanceRequest.Version` is set the gRPC StartInstance handler does not copy it onto the constructed event.

## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

N/A

## Release Note

<!-- How should the fix for this issue be communicated in our release notes? It can be populated later. -->
<!-- Keep it as a single line. Examples: -->

<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **ADD** New feature in Dapr. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Bug in runtime. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **UPDATE** Runtime dependency. -->

RELEASE NOTE:

comments.body: I think the version in the `ExecutionStartedEvent` is not used. There's `version` in other events and AFAIK we're not using. I think we should deprecate them, but we need to confirm first.
